working_on: Audit-time external-source fetching ADR + fetch_structural_reference.py tool — Option A carve-out per the user-adjudicated plan at ~/.claude/plans/i-want-an-adversarial-toasty-storm.md.
declared_scope: Author new engine ADR (audit-time-structural-reference-fetching) authorizing bounded ephemeral fetching of public Tier 4 structural references for audit-time comparison-not-extraction; preserves ADR 0011 ceiling and ADR 0046 authoring posture. Author paired tool engine/tools/fetch_structural_reference.py composing the existing parser + extending S-0103 source-identity anonymization to URL-fetched briefs + polite-fetcher discipline (User-Agent, robots.txt, rate-limit, ephemeral cache). Author tests (engine/tools/test_fetch_structural_reference.py). Amend ADR 0047 Consequences with paired-tool back-pointer; amend phase_5_production_audit.md methodology with optional empirical-fortification branch for medium-confidence verdicts; amend product/docs/content-strategy.md Tier 4 with per-source fetch-policy column; register in engine/operations/cross-references.md; author engine/build_readiness/fetch_structural_reference_first_exercise.md. MemPalace decision + lesson capture.

scope_delivery.explanation: All declared deliverables landed: ADR 0059 + paired tool fetch_structural_reference.py + 27 tests (all passing; existing 155 parser tests still pass) + ADR 0047 Consequences amendment + Phase 5 audit methodology optional empirical-fortification branch + content-strategy.md Tier 4 fetch-policy table + engine/operations/cross-references.md ADR 0059 row + engine/adr/README.md index row + voluntary first-exercise gate report (ADR 0053 strict trigger does not fire; voluntary application per trigger-when-in-doubt bias) + MemPalace decision drawer (paideia/decisions) + lesson drawer (paideia/lessons) + diary entry. No descope, no reorder, no deferral mid-session. The first exercise of the new mechanism — fortifying S-0104 medium-confidence verdicts against real SEP fetches — lands in a future interactive audit-closeout session per ADR 0053 (T1-A through T1-D in the readiness note close at that exercise). User adjudication captured in approved-plan file.

outcome_summary: Authored ADR 0059 (audit-time fetching of public Tier 4 structural references for verdict fortification — Option A carve-out from the adversarial-analysis plan) + paired tool fetch_structural_reference.py composing the existing parser via select_adapter + extract + extract_entries + emit_focusing_brief + serialize_brief; the parser's anti-fetch contract is preserved verbatim. The S-0103 source-identity anonymization helpers are reused via direct function import (no fork) and the recursive-walk anonymization invariant gates the URL-fetched brief before return (exit code 6 on violation). 27 new tests cover lifecycle / robots.txt / rate-limit / network failure-and-retry / anonymization invariant / composition round-trip / CLI exit codes; existing 155 parser tests still pass. ADR 0047 Consequences amended in-place. Phase 5 audit methodology gains an optional empirical-fortification branch for medium-confidence verdicts that imply graph mutations. content-strategy.md Tier 4 gains a per-host fetch-policy table (SEP / IEP / Wikipedia authorized; Routledge / Oxford Reference out of scope, paywalled). cross-references.md registers the new ADR. Voluntary build-readiness first-exercise note authored despite ADR 0053 strict trigger not firing — the master-plan amendment routes routine-mode audit sessions to the new tool, so the gate's spirit applies. ADR 0011 ceiling and ADR 0046 authoring-time posture are unchanged; ADR 0057 fresh-content-probe principle is operationalized for graph audits. Validator at shutdown: 0 hard-fails; 371 soft-warns (baseline carryovers — 366 missing_rigor_score Phase-5 carryover + 4 issue_collision pre-existing + 1 adr_consequences_deliverable_audit pre-existing). Three commits beyond eager-claim.
